e: Tóxico is an adjective meaning 'toxic' or 'poisonous.' It describes substances or situations that are harmful. Example: 'pesticidas tóxicos' means 'toxic pesticides.'
Formidable is an adjective meaning 'formidable' or 'tremendous,' describing someone or something impressive and powerful. Example: 'el hombre era muy formidable' means 'the man was very formidable.'
Legalmente is an adverb meaning 'legally' or 'in accordance with law.' Example: 'se comprometió legalmente' means 'he legally committed himself.'
Ecosistema is a noun meaning 'ecosystem'—a community of living organisms interacting with their physical environment. Example: 'ecosistemas tan frágiles' means 'very fragile ecosystems.'
Internar is a verb meaning 'to confine' or 'to shut up' (animals in a place). Example: 'internó los perros al patio' means 'he confined the dogs to the patio.'
Ráfaga is a noun meaning a 'gust' (of wind) or 'burst' (of gunfire). Example: 'las ráfagas de viento' means 'gusts of wind.'

Manchado is an adjective meaning 'stained' or 'dirty,' often used metaphorically for moral corruption. Example: 'aquellos cuyas manos se han manchado de sangre' means 'those whose hands have been stained with blood.'
Mecánica is a noun meaning 'mechanics' (the science) or 'mechanism' (a system). Example: 'mediante la mecánica de Newton' means 'using Newton's mechanics.'
Inclusión is a noun meaning 'inclusion'—the act of including or the state of being included. Example: 'la inclusión de niños especiales' means 'the inclusion of special needs children.'

s: apto means 'appropriate, fit, able' in Spanish
e: Apto is an adjective meaning 'appropriate,' 'fit,' or 'able'—describing someone/something that has the right qualities. Example: 'apto para modelar sonidos' means 'able to shape sounds.'
Restante is an adjective meaning 'remaining' or 'leftover.' Example: 'los restantes socios' means 'the remaining partners.'
Fronterizo is an adjective meaning 'border' or 'relating to a border.' Example: 'la zona fronteriza' means 'the border zone.'
Encierro is a noun meaning 'confinement' or 'imprisonment'—literally being locked in. Example: 'tras 7 años de encierro' means 'after 7 years of incarceration.'
Prevalecer is a verb meaning 'to prevail'—to be dominant or victorious. Example: 'hacen prevalecer la razón' means 'they make reason prevail.'
Cheque is a noun meaning 'check'—a written order to a bank to pay money. Example: 'un cheque puede ser endosado' means 'a check can be endorsed.'
Dudoso is an adjective meaning 'doubtful' or 'dubious'—expressing uncertainty or skepticism. Example: 'estaba dudoso de que fuera la historia real' means 'he was doubtful that it was the real story.'

s: postular means 'to postulate, nominate' in Spanish
e: Postular is a verb meaning 'to postulate' (propose a theory) or 'to nominate' (propose a person for office). Example: 'no se podía postular a este personaje' means 'you couldn't nominate this character.'
Portador is a noun meaning 'bearer' or 'carrier'—someone who carries or bears something. Example: 'era el portador de buenas noticias' means 'he was the bearer of good news.'
Confederación is a noun meaning 'confederation'—a political union of states or groups. Example: 'las leyes de la Confederación' means 'the laws of the Confederation.'
Film is a noun meaning 'film' or 'movie'—a motion picture. Example: 'repaso las películas para determinar cual es el mejor film' means 'I'm reviewing the movies to determine which is the best film.'
Portavoz is a noun meaning 'spokesperson'—someone who speaks on behalf of a group. Example: 'el portavoz del presidente' means 'the president's spokesman.'
Compensación is a noun meaning 'compensation'—payment for loss or damage. Example: 'ninguna compensación alcanzará nuestros esfuerzos' means 'no compensation will make up for our efforts.'
Temática is a noun meaning 'theme' or 'thematic content'—the main subject or topic. Example: 'la temática es muy interesante' means 'the theme is very interesting.'
Noroeste is a noun meaning 'northwest'—the cardinal direction between north and west. Example: 'lluvia fuerte sobre el noroeste' means 'heavy rain over the northwest.'
Jinete is a noun meaning 'rider' or 'horseman'—someone who rides a horse. Example: 'el jinete cae de un caballo' means 'the rider falls from a horse.'
Pertenencia is a noun meaning 'membership' or 'the state of belonging.' Example: 'su pertenencia a la asociación' means 'his membership in the association.'
Estrellar is a verb meaning 'to crash' or 'to smash.' Example: 'se estrelló en un accidente' means 'he got smashed up in an accident.'
Pasivo is an adjective meaning 'passive'—not active or reactive. Example: 'mi abuelo siempre fue pasivo' means 'my grandfather was always passive.'

s: percatar means 'to inform, become aware' in Spanish
e: Percatar is a verb meaning 'to become aware' or 'to notice.' Example: 'se percatarse de nuestras intenciones' means 'to become aware of our intentions.'

s: cordón means 'cord, yarn, braid' in Spanish
e: Cordón is a noun meaning 'cord,' 'rope,' or 'braid.' Example: 'el cordón umbilical' means 'the umbilical cord.'
Melancolía is a noun meaning 'melancholy'—a deep sadness or pensiveness. Example: 'exceso de tristeza y melancolía' means 'excessive sadness and melancholy.'
Arrodillar is a verb meaning 'to kneel'—to go down on one's knees. Example: 'puedo arrodillarme humildemente' means 'I can kneel humbly.'

s: plancha means '(electric) iron, plate, sheet' in Spanish
e: Plancha is a noun meaning 'iron' (for pressing clothes), 'plate,' or 'sheet' (of metal). Example: 'con la plancha caliente' means 'with the hot iron.'

s: dispersar means 'to disperse, scatter, spread' in Spanish
e: Dispersar is a verb meaning 'to disperse' or 'scatter'—to spread things in different directions. Example: 'la energía dispersa en el espacio' means 'energy scattered into space.'
Artesano is a noun meaning 'artisan' or 'craftsman'—someone who makes things by hand with skill. Example: 'buenos diseñadores pero mediocres artesanos' means 'good designers but mediocre craftsmen.'
Movilidad is a noun meaning 'mobility'—the ability to move or be moved. Example: 'escasa movilidad social' means 'insufficient social mobility.'
Zapatilla is a noun meaning 'slipper' or 'shoe'—casual footwear. Example: 'sus zapatillas Nike' means 'her Nike shoes.'
Intuición is a noun meaning 'intuition'—immediate understanding without conscious reasoning. Example: 'la intuición de las madres no falla' means 'a mother's intuition never fails.'

s: consigna means 'watchword, motto, slogan' in Spanish
e: Consigna is a noun meaning 'watchword,' 'motto,' or 'slogan'—a rallying cry or guiding principle. Example: 'la consigna mosquetera' means 'the musketeer motto.'
Casero is an adjective meaning 'homemade' or 'homey'—made at home rather than commercially. Example: 'buena comida casera' means 'good homemade food.'
Credibilidad is a noun meaning 'credibility'—the quality of being believable. Example: 'No tiene credibilidad' means 'It has no credibility.'

s: pequeñito means 'very small, tiny' in Spanish
e: Pequeñito is a diminutive adjective meaning 'very small' or 'tiny'—smaller than 'pequeño.' Example: 'una casa bien pequeñita' means 'a really tiny house.'
Narrativo is an adjective meaning 'narrative'—relating to telling a story. Example: 'libros de poesía y narrativa' means 'books of poetry and narrative/prose.'
Constructor is a noun meaning 'builder'—someone who constructs buildings or structures. Example: 'arquitectos, constructores y otros' means 'architects, builders, and others.'
Térmico is an adjective meaning 'thermal'—relating to heat or temperature. Example: 'energía térmica' means 'thermal energy.'
Poblador is a noun meaning 'settler' or 'resident'—someone who lives in or settles a place. Example: 'mil millones de pobladores urbanos' means 'a billion urban residents.'
Bienvenida is a noun meaning 'welcome'—a greeting or reception. Example: 'la más cordial bienvenida' means 'the most cordial welcome.'
Descuido is a noun meaning 'neglect,' 'oversight,' or 'carelessness.' Example: 'al menor descuido' means 'with the smallest distraction.'

Subjetivo refers to something based on individual opinion or perspective rather than objective facts. It comes from the Latin 'subjectum' meaning 'placed under' and describes things dependent on a person's point of view.

s: This Spanish adjective relates to molecules or the smallest units of chemical substances.
q: What Spanish word is related to "atomic"?
e: Molecular refers to molecules or the molecular level of matter. It describes properties or structures related to molecules, the smallest units of chemical substances.
Aislamiento means the state of being isolated or cut off from others. It can refer to physical separation, political isolation, or a feeling of loneliness.
Expulsión is the act of forcing someone to leave a place, institution, or country. It's a formal and permanent removal.
Atributo refers to a characteristic or quality that belongs to or describes someone or something. It's a defining feature or property.
Alarmar means to cause worry or fear in someone, to make them anxious about danger or a serious situation.

s: This Spanish noun refers to a deep feeling of regret, sorrow, or grief.
q: What Spanish word is related to "regret"?
e: Lamento refers to a deep feeling of regret, sorrow, or mourning. It can be expressed through words or cries of grief.
Garra refers to the sharp curved nail or claw of an animal, particularly birds and wild beasts. It's also used metaphorically for a person's aggressive power.
Rotundo describes something that is absolute, emphatic, and clear-cut. It originally referred to roundness but now means categorical or unambiguous.
Delirio refers to a mental state of confusion and wild excitement, often caused by fever or extreme stress. It involves hallucinations or incoherent thoughts.
Respiratorio refers to the respiratory system—the organs and processes involved in breathing and gas exchange.
Bodega refers to a cellar, storehouse, or wine repository. In modern Spanish, it also means a small grocery shop, especially in Spanish-speaking communities.

s: This Spanish noun describes a tendency to believe that positive outcomes will occur.
q: What Spanish word is related to "hope"?
e: Optimismo is the tendency to believe that good things will happen and that situations will have positive outcomes.
Congregación refers to a gathered group of people, especially members of a religious community or church.
Accesible means easy to reach, enter, or use. It describes something that is available to everyone, including people with disabilities.
Exceder means to go beyond a limit, to surpass a boundary, or to be greater than expected or allowed.

s: This Spanish noun refers to all the plants in a particular region or ecosystem.
q: What Spanish word is related to "plants"?
e: Flora refers to all the plants in a particular region or ecosystem. It's also the Roman goddess of flowers.
Monasterio is a building or complex where monks live and worship. It's a religious community dedicated to spiritual life.
Cuerno refers to the hard projection on an animal's head, or can mean a wind instrument made from animal horn.
Ajedrez is the ancient game of chess, played between two opponents with pieces on a checkered board.

s: This Spanish word can mean either work done by hand or a book of instructions.
q: What Spanish word is related to "handbook"?
e: Manual refers to something done by hand or an instruction guide. It can describe both physical work and a book of instructions.
Diosa is a female deity or goddess. It's the feminine form of 'dios' (god) in Spanish.
Óptimo means the best possible or most favorable condition. It describes the ideal state or highest quality.
Polémico describes something that causes or is likely to cause controversy or heated debate.
Firmeza refers to the quality of being firm, solid, or unwavering. It can describe both physical hardness and mental resolve.
Narrativa refers to the art of storytelling or a narrative structure. It's the way a story is told.
Ropero refers to a large cupboard or wardrobe where clothes are stored, or the collection of clothes themselves.
Bombero is a firefighter—someone who fights fires and responds to fire emergencies.
Guardián refers to a person who guards or protects someone, especially a legal guardian or guardian angel.
Relajar means to make or become relaxed, to loosen tension, or to rest.
Deteriorar means to become worse or to cause something to become worse in quality or condition.
Liviano means light in weight, or figuratively describes something that is frivolous or of little substance.
Inquietar means to cause someone to feel uneasy, worried, or disturbed about something.
Maestría refers to great skill and expertise, or a master's degree program. It describes mastery of a subject or craft.
Lentitud refers to the quality of moving or happening slowly. It describes a state of being unhurried.
Adopción is the legal process of taking a child into one's family as a full member, or adopting something new.
Hispanoamericano refers to the peoples, cultures, and literature of Spanish-speaking America and their combination with English-speaking culture.
Feroz describes something that is fierce, wild, or ferocious—showing intense aggression or savagery.
Acusado refers to a person who is accused of a crime and is defending themselves in court.
Ilícito describes something that is illegal or forbidden by law. It refers to unlawful or improper activities.
Ambicioso describes someone who has a strong desire for success, power, or achievement.
Ataúd is a wooden box or case in which a dead body is placed for burial.
Sostenible describes something that can be maintained or continued indefinitely without depleting resources.
Estadístico refers to statistics or the analysis of numerical data to understand patterns and correlations.
Difícilmente means 'hardly' or 'scarcely,' indicating that something is difficult to accomplish or unlikely to happen.
Consola refers to a gaming console—an electronic device connected to a TV for playing video games.
Crujir means to make a sharp crackling or crunching sound, like breaking something brittle.
Vagar means to wander aimlessly or to travel from place to place without a fixed destination.
Cosita is a diminutive form of 'cosa' (thing), referring to something small, cute, or unimportant.
Armario is a large cupboard or closet where clothes and personal items are stored.
cosita is a Spanish word meaning "little thing".
armario is a Spanish word meaning "closet, cupboard".
